values: The kitchen is bare and there are mouths to feed before sundown
A celebration is coming and the cook has nothing to work with
The tavern ran out of supplies and the regulars are getting restless
The provisions for the road crew spoiled. They need fresh stock today
A trade delegation is arriving and we have nothing to serve them
The cook left and took half the pantry. We are starting over
The settlement's food stores were stretched thin after hosting refugees
There is a sick child who needs a particular broth and we lack the ingredients
The baker's supplies were ruined when the storehouse roof leaked
Market day is coming and every stall needs to be stocked or we lose trade
The inn cannot serve meals without basic provisions. It is embarrassing
We promised rations to the patrol and cannot go back on our word
Someone underestimated how much the workers eat. The shortfall is significant
The ferry workers expect a hot meal at the end of their shift. No exceptions
Feeding the settlement is not heroic work. But it is necessary work
